comment: This is generated by ESQL's TStepTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tstep
description:
Groups `@timestamp` into fixed-width time buckets for aggregation, labeling each bucket by its end instant.



The grid is anchored using the **start** of the `@timestamp` range from the request query filter
(see TimestampBoundsAware). `TSTEP` is not compatible with `TRANGE` in the same query at the moment.



TBucket aligns buckets to calendar boundaries or an epoch-based grid in the configured time zone.
`TSTEP` uses a contiguous `step`-wide grid in UTC and returns end-labeled bucket timestamps.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: to

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` and rounded up so the target bucket count is not exceeded. TSTEP always needs a range to anchor the grid; when `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(2, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T14:15:00.000Z")
| SORT bucket
